item.title: George Martin
item.description: Era: 1950s–1998 (primary period: 1962–1970) Genres: Pop rock, Merseybeat, psychedelic pop, art rock, baroque pop Key artists: The Beatles RYM · Apple Music · Spotify George Martin signed the Beatles because nobody else would, and the records they made together over the next eight years changed what ...

item.title: Phil Spector
item.description: Era: Late 1950s–1970s (primary period: 1961–1966) Genres: Girl group, Pop soul, Rock & roll, pop rock Key artists: The Ronettes, The Crystals, The Righteous Brothers, The Beatles, Ike & Tina Turner RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Phil Spector is the first pop producer to matter as an auteur, the...

item.title: The Funk Brothers
item.description: Location: Hitsville U.S.A., Detroit, Michigan Period: 1959–1972 Key members: James Jamerson (bass), Benny Benjamin (drums), Earl Van Dyke (keyboards), Robert White (guitar), Eddie Willis (guitar), Joe Messina (guitar), Jack Ashford (percussion), Uriel Jones (drums) RYM The most prolific and least cr...

item.title: Burt Bacharach
item.description: Genres: Pop soul, traditional pop, film score Scene: Brill Building Era: 1957–2023 RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Burt Bacharach is the most harmonically sophisticated songwriter in the history of American pop — the figure who brought Ravel, Debussy, and Brazilian bossa nova into the three-minute singl...

item.title: Gerry Goffin
item.description: Era: 1960s–1990s (primary period: 1960–1967) Genres: Girl group, Pop soul, Rock & roll, soul Key collaborator: Carole King (co-writer and wife, 1959–1968) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Gerry Goffin was the lyricist of the most prolific and versatile songwriting team in the Brill Building — the par...

item.title: Hal David
item.description: Era: 1950s–2000s (primary period: 1957–1973) Genres: Pop soul, traditional pop, Brill Building Key collaborator: Burt Bacharach (co-writer, 1957–1973) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Hal David wrote lyrics that sound simple until you try to write one yourself.

item.title: Holland-Dozier-Holland
item.description: Era: 1962–1967 (Motown); 1968–present (independent) Genres: Soul, Pop soul, Girl group Key collaborators: The Supremes, The Four Tops, The Temptations, Martha and the Vandellas RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The most prolific and commercially successful songwriting-production team in the history of Mot...

item.title: Jeff Barry
item.description: Era: 1960s–1970s (primary period: 1961–1966) Genres: Girl group, Pop soul, Rock & roll Key collaborator: Ellie Greenwich (co-writer and wife, 1962–1965) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Jeff Barry was the Brill Building’s most instinctive hitmaker — the songwriter who understood, more clearly than an...
